15	"""Externalized model-weight helpers for checkpoint exports."""
16	
17	import json
18	import logging
19	import os
20	import re
21	
22	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
23	
24	EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_FFN = "int4_ffn"
25	EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_MOE = "int4_moe"
26	EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_NVFP4_MOE = "nvfp4_moe"
27	EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_LM_HEAD = "lm_head"
28	EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_ALL = "all"
29	EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_KINDS = (
30	    EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_FFN,
31	    EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_MOE,
32	    EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_NVFP4_MOE,
33	    EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_LM_HEAD,
34	)
35	EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_CHOICES = (*EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_KINDS, EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_ALL)
36	
37	
38	def resolve_externalize_weights(externalize_weights) -> "list[str]":
39	    """Normalize and validate requested external weight kinds."""
40	    if externalize_weights is None:
41	        return []
42	    if isinstance(externalize_weights, str):
43	        values = [externalize_weights]
44	    else:
45	        values = list(externalize_weights)
46	
47	    requested: list[str] = []
48	    for value in values:
49	        if value == EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_ALL:
50	            return list(EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_KINDS)
51	        if value not in EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_KINDS:
52	            supported = ", ".join(EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_CHOICES)
53	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported external weight kind '{value}'. "
54	                             f"Supported kinds: {supported}")
55	        if value not in requested:
56	            requested.append(value)
57	    return requested
58	
59	
60	def patch_external_weight_manifest(
61	        out_dir: str,
62	        external_weight_files: "list[dict[str, object]]") -> None:
63	    """Add external weight file metadata to runtime ``config.json``."""
64	    config_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "config.json")
65	    with open(config_path) as f:
66	        config = json.load(f)
67	    config["external_weight_files"] = external_weight_files
68	    with open(config_path, "w") as f:
69	        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
70	    logger.info("Patched config.json with %d external weight file(s)",
71	                len(external_weight_files))
72	
73	
74	def reject_quantized_lm_head_externalization(
75	        model, model_dir: str,
76	        requested_external_weights: "list[str]") -> None:
77	    """Refuse ``--externalize-weights lm_head`` when the LM head is quantized.
78	
79	    The LM-head externalization path assumes a single dense fp16 weight
80	    initializer it can move into a safetensors external weight file.  When the
81	    LM head is quantized (FP8 / NVFP4 / INT4 AWQ / ...), error out here before
82	    the expensive ONNX export and tell the user to drop ``lm_head`` from
83	    ``--externalize-weights`` or re-quantize without ``--lm_head_quantization``.
84	
85	    The check delegates to :func:`config.module_quant_type`, which encodes
86	    the same lm_head decision :func:`models.linear.make_linear` uses
87	    (``quant.excluded``, tied embeddings, MIXED_PRECISION layer overrides,
88	    dominant ``quant_type``), so detection automatically tracks any future
89	    ``hf_quant_config.json`` shapes that the parser learns to handle.
90	    """
91	    if EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_LM_HEAD not in requested_external_weights:
92	        return
93	
94	    from .config import QUANT_FP16, module_quant_type
95	
96	    lm_head_quant = module_quant_type("lm_head", model.config)
97	    if lm_head_quant == QUANT_FP16:
98	        return
99	
100	    location = f" at {model_dir!r}" if model_dir else ""
101	    raise ValueError(
102	        f"--externalize-weights lm_head requires an fp16 LM head, but the "
103	        f"checkpoint{location} has lm_head quantized as {lm_head_quant!r}. "
104	        "Re-quantize without --lm_head_quantization, or omit 'lm_head' "
105	        "from --externalize-weights.")
106	
107	
108	def _is_dense_ffn_int4_weight_name(
109	        name: str, layer_types: "list[str] | tuple[str, ...]") -> bool:
110	    """Return True when ``name`` belongs to a dense FFN int4 plugin input.
111	
112	    The attention Q/K/V/O projections also use Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin; keep
113	    them embedded in ONNX for now.  Dense decoder FFNs use ``*.mlp.*`` names in
114	    the default loader path.  Nemotron-H uses ``backbone.layers.N.mixer`` for
115	    both attention and MLP-only layers, so check ``layer_types`` before marking
116	    those weights external.
117	    """
118	    normalized = name.replace("/", ".").lower()
119	    ffn_projections = (".gate_proj.", ".up_proj.", ".down_proj.")
120	    if ".mlp." in normalized and any(p in normalized for p in ffn_projections):
121	        return True
122	
123	    match = re.search(
124	        r"backbone\.layers\.(\d+)\.mixer\.(up_proj|down_proj)\.",
125	        normalized,
126	    )
127	    if match is None:
128	        return False
129	
130	    layer_idx = int(match.group(1))
131	    return layer_idx < len(layer_types) and layer_types[layer_idx] == "mlp"
132	
133	
134	def _find_int4_ffn_weight_initializers(onnx_model, model) -> "list[str]":
135	    """Return dense FFN Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin qweight/scale initializers."""
136	    initializers = {init.name for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer}
137	    layer_types = tuple(getattr(model.config, "layer_types", ()))
138	
139	    external_names: list[str] = []
140	    external_name_set: set[str] = set()
141	    for node in onnx_model.graph.node:
142	        if node.domain != "trt_edgellm" or node.op_type != "Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin":
143	            continue
144	        if len(node.input) < 3:
145	            continue
146	        if not _is_dense_ffn_int4_weight_name(node.input[1], layer_types):
147	            continue
148	        for tensor_name in (node.input[1], node.input[2]):
149	            if tensor_name in initializers and tensor_name not in external_name_set:
150	                external_names.append(tensor_name)
151	                external_name_set.add(tensor_name)
152	    return external_names
153	
154	
155	def _find_int4_moe_weight_initializers(onnx_model) -> "list[str]":
156	    """Return Int4MoePlugin qweight initializers, leaving scales embedded."""
157	    initializers = {init.name for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer}
158	
159	    external_names: list[str] = []
160	    external_name_set: set[str] = set()
161	    for node in onnx_model.graph.node:
162	        if node.domain != "trt_edgellm" or node.op_type != "Int4MoePlugin":
163	            continue
164	        if len(node.input) < 5:
165	            continue
166	        for tensor_name in (node.input[2], node.input[4]):
167	            if tensor_name in initializers and tensor_name not in external_name_set:
168	                external_names.append(tensor_name)
169	                external_name_set.add(tensor_name)
170	    return external_names
171	
172	
173	def _find_nvfp4_moe_weight_initializers(onnx_model) -> "list[str]":
174	    """Return NVFP4 MoE plugin initializer inputs."""
175	    initializers = {init.name for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer}
176	    plugin_op_types = {"Nvfp4MoePlugin", "NvFP4MoEPluginGeforce"}
177	
178	    external_names: list[str] = []
179	    external_name_set: set[str] = set()
180	    for node in onnx_model.graph.node:
181	        if node.domain != "trt_edgellm" or node.op_type not in plugin_op_types:
182	            continue
183	        for tensor_name in node.input:
184	            if tensor_name in initializers and tensor_name not in external_name_set:
185	                external_names.append(tensor_name)
186	                external_name_set.add(tensor_name)
187	    return external_names
188	
189	
190	def _lm_head_weight_candidate_shapes(model) -> "set[tuple[int, ...]]":
191	    """Return plausible LM-head shapes: actual weight first, then config vocab/hidden fallbacks."""
192	    shapes: set[tuple[int, ...]] = set()
193	
194	    lm_head = getattr(model, "lm_head", None)
195	    weight = getattr(lm_head, "weight", None)
196	    if weight is not None:
197	        shapes.add(tuple(int(dim) for dim in weight.shape))
198	
199	    config = getattr(model, "config", None)
200	    hidden_size = getattr(config, "hidden_size", None)
201	    vocab_sizes = [
202	        getattr(config, "reduced_vocab_size", None),
203	        # EAGLE3 draft heads project to a reduced draft vocabulary; the ONNX
204	        # MatMul weight is then (hidden, draft_vocab), which the vocab_size /
205	        # reduced_vocab_size fallbacks alone do not cover.
206	        getattr(config, "draft_vocab_size", None),
207	        getattr(config, "vocab_size", None),
208	    ]
209	    for vocab_size in vocab_sizes:
210	        if hidden_size is None or vocab_size is None:
211	            continue
212	        hidden = int(hidden_size)
213	        vocab = int(vocab_size)
214	        shapes.add((vocab, hidden))
215	        shapes.add((hidden, vocab))
216	    return shapes
217	
218	
219	def _find_initializer_from_value(
220	        value_name: str, initializers: dict, producer_by_output: dict,
221	        candidate_shapes: "set[tuple[int, ...]]") -> str:
222	    """Trace simple view/cast values back to a candidate initializer."""
223	    if value_name in initializers:
224	        init = initializers[value_name]
225	        if tuple(init.dims) in candidate_shapes:
226	            return value_name
227	        return ""
228	
229	    producer = producer_by_output.get(value_name)
230	    if producer is None or producer.op_type not in ("Cast", "Identity",
231	                                                    "Transpose"):
232	        return ""
233	
234	    for input_name in producer.input:
235	        tensor_name = _find_initializer_from_value(input_name, initializers,
236	                                                   producer_by_output,
237	                                                   candidate_shapes)
238	        if tensor_name:
239	            return tensor_name
240	    return ""
241	
242	
243	def _find_lm_head_weight_initializer(onnx_model, model) -> "str | None":
244	    """Return the ONNX initializer that backs the final LM-head projection."""
245	    initializers = {init.name: init for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer}
246	    candidate_shapes = _lm_head_weight_candidate_shapes(model)
247	    if not candidate_shapes:
248	        return None
249	
250	    shape_matches = [
251	        init.name for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer
252	        if tuple(init.dims) in candidate_shapes
253	    ]
254	    named_matches = [
255	        name for name in shape_matches
256	        if "lm_head" in name.replace("/", ".").lower()
257	    ]
258	    if len(named_matches) == 1:
259	        return named_matches[0]
260	    if len(shape_matches) == 1:
261	        return shape_matches[0]
262	
263	    producer_by_output = {
264	        output_name: node
265	        for node in onnx_model.graph.node
266	        for output_name in node.output
267	    }
268	
269	    # Start at the public logits output.  Exporters commonly insert a final
270	    # Cast or Identity, so walk through those before looking for the Gemm/MatMul
271	    # that implements the LM-head projection.
272	    logits_outputs = [
273	        output.name for output in onnx_model.graph.output
274	        if output.name == "logits"
275	    ]
276	    worklist = logits_outputs or [
277	        output.name for output in onnx_model.graph.output
278	    ]
279	    seen_values: set[str] = set()
280	    # LogSoftmax appears between logits and the head MatMul for EAGLE3 draft
281	    # models (which emit log-probs); treat it as a passthrough so the walk
282	    # reaches the projection weight.
283	    passthrough_ops = {
284	        "Cast", "Identity", "Reshape", "Transpose", "LogSoftmax"
285	    }
286	
287	    while worklist:
288	        value_name = worklist.pop()
289	        if value_name in seen_values:
290	            continue
291	        seen_values.add(value_name)
292	
293	        node = producer_by_output.get(value_name)
294	        if node is None:
295	            continue
296	
297	        # For the final projection, the weight is normally the second Gemm or
298	        # MatMul input.  It can either be an initializer directly or the input
299	        # to a small Transpose inserted by ONNX export for torch.nn.Linear.
300	        if node.op_type in ("Gemm", "MatMul"):
301	            for input_name in list(node.input)[1:]:
302	                tensor_name = _find_initializer_from_value(
303	                    input_name, initializers, producer_by_output,
304	                    candidate_shapes)
305	                if tensor_name:
306	                    return tensor_name
307	            continue
308	
309	        if node.op_type in passthrough_ops:
310	            worklist.extend(node.input[:1])
311	            continue
312	
313	        if node.op_type == "Add":
314	            worklist.extend(input_name for input_name in node.input
315	                            if input_name not in initializers)
316	
317	    if len(named_matches) > 1:
318	        logger.warning(
319	            "Multiple lm_head-shaped initializers matched by name: %s",
320	            named_matches)
321	    elif len(shape_matches) > 1:
322	        logger.warning("Multiple LM-head-shaped initializers found: %s",
323	                       shape_matches)
324	    return None
325	
326	
327	def _write_external_weight_file(out_dir: str, external_weight_file: str,
328	                                tensor_names: "list[str]",
329	                                initializers: dict) -> str:
330	    """Write selected ONNX initializers to a safetensors external weight file."""
331	    import numpy as np
332	    import onnx
333	    import torch
334	    from safetensors.torch import save_file
335	
336	    external_weight_tensors = {}
337	    for tensor_name in tensor_names:
338	        init = initializers[tensor_name]
339	        array = onnx.numpy_helper.to_array(init)
340	        external_weight_tensors[tensor_name] = torch.from_numpy(
341	            np.array(array, copy=True))
342	    external_weight_path = os.path.join(out_dir, external_weight_file)
343	    save_file(external_weight_tensors, external_weight_path)
344	    return external_weight_path
345	
346	
347	def _add_external_weight_inputs(onnx_model, tensor_names: "list[str]",
348	                                initializers: dict) -> None:
349	    """Expose selected initializers as fixed-shape ONNX graph inputs."""
350	    import onnx
351	
352	    graph_input_names = {inp.name for inp in onnx_model.graph.input}
353	    for tensor_name in tensor_names:
354	        if tensor_name not in graph_input_names:
355	            init = initializers[tensor_name]
356	            onnx_model.graph.input.append(
357	                onnx.helper.make_tensor_value_info(
358	                    tensor_name,
359	                    init.data_type,
360	                    list(init.dims),
361	                ))
362	            graph_input_names.add(tensor_name)
363	
364	
365	def _remove_externalized_initializers(onnx_model,
366	                                      tensor_names: "list[str]") -> None:
367	    """Remove selected tensors from graph initializers after adding inputs."""
368	    external_name_set = set(tensor_names)
369	    retained_initializers = [
370	        init for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer
371	        if init.name not in external_name_set
372	    ]
373	    del onnx_model.graph.initializer[:]
374	    onnx_model.graph.initializer.extend(retained_initializers)
375	
376	
377	def _save_externalized_onnx_model(onnx_path: str, onnx_model) -> None:
378	    """Save ONNX after all requested externalization passes are complete."""
379	    import onnx
380	
381	    out_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(onnx_path))
382	    ext_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "model.onnx.data")
383	    if os.path.isfile(ext_path):
384	        old_size = os.path.getsize(ext_path)
385	        logger.info("Removing stale external data %s (%.2f GB) before re-save",
386	                    ext_path, old_size / 1e9)
387	        os.remove(ext_path)
388	    onnx.save_model(
389	        onnx_model,
390	        onnx_path,
391	        save_as_external_data=True,
392	        all_tensors_to_one_file=True,
393	        location="model.onnx.data",
394	        convert_attribute=True,
395	    )
396	
397	
398	def externalize_model_weights(
399	        onnx_path: str,
400	        model,
401	        externalize_weights=None) -> "list[dict[str, object]]":
402	    """Move requested ONNX initializer weights into external weight files."""
403	    import onnx
404	
405	    requested = resolve_externalize_weights(externalize_weights)
406	    if not requested:
407	        return []
408	
409	    onnx_model = onnx.load(onnx_path)
410	    initializers = {init.name: init for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer}
411	    out_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(onnx_path))
412	
413	    manifest: list[dict[str, object]] = []
414	    external_names: list[str] = []
415	    external_name_set: set[str] = set()
416	
417	    def add_external_weight_file(kind: str, external_weight_file: str,
418	                                 tensor_names: "list[str]",
419	                                 manifest_kind: str) -> None:
420	        new_names = [
421	            name for name in tensor_names if name not in external_name_set
422	        ]
423	        if not new_names:
424	            return
425	        external_weight_path = _write_external_weight_file(
426	            out_dir, external_weight_file, new_names, initializers)
427	        external_names.extend(new_names)
428	        external_name_set.update(new_names)
429	        manifest.append({
430	            "file": external_weight_file,
431	            "kind": manifest_kind,
432	            "tensors": new_names,
433	        })
434	        logger.info("Externalized %d %s tensor(s) to %s", len(new_names), kind,
435	                    external_weight_path)
436	
437	    for kind in requested:
438	        if kind == EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_FFN:
439	            names = _find_int4_ffn_weight_initializers(onnx_model, model)
440	            if not names:
441	                logger.warning(
442	                    "External int4_ffn weights requested, but no dense FFN "
443	                    "Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin initializers were found")
444	                continue
445	            add_external_weight_file("FFN Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin",
446	                                     "external_int4_ffn_weights.safetensors",
447	                                     names, "int4_ffn_weights")
448	            continue
449	
450	        if kind == EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_MOE:
451	            names = _find_int4_moe_weight_initializers(onnx_model)
452	            if not names:
453	                logger.warning("External int4_moe weights requested, but no "
454	                               "Int4MoePlugin qweight initializers were found")
455	                continue
456	            add_external_weight_file("Int4MoePlugin qweight",
457	                                     "external_int4_moe_weights.safetensors",
458	                                     names, "int4_moe_weights")
459	            continue
460	
461	        if kind == EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_NVFP4_MOE:
462	            names = _find_nvfp4_moe_weight_initializers(onnx_model)
463	            if not names:
464	                logger.warning(
465	                    "External nvfp4_moe weights requested, but no "
466	                    "NVFP4 MoE plugin initializer inputs were found")
467	                continue
468	            add_external_weight_file("NVFP4 MoE plugin",
469	                                     "external_nvfp4_moe_weights.safetensors",
470	                                     names, "nvfp4_moe_weights")
471	            continue
472	
473	        if kind == EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_LM_HEAD:
474	            tensor_name = _find_lm_head_weight_initializer(onnx_model, model)
475	            if tensor_name is None:
476	                logger.warning(
477	                    "External lm_head weight requested, but no unique "
478	                    "LM-head initializer was found")
479	                continue
480	            add_external_weight_file("LM-head weight",
481	                                     "external_lm_head_weight.safetensors",
482	                                     [tensor_name], "lm_head_weight")
483	
484	    if not external_names:
485	        return []
486	
487	    _add_external_weight_inputs(onnx_model, external_names, initializers)
488	    _remove_externalized_initializers(onnx_model, external_names)
489	    _save_externalized_onnx_model(onnx_path, onnx_model)
490	    return manifest

# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
"""Externalized model-weight helpers for checkpoint exports."""

import json
import logging
import os
import re

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_FFN = "int4_ffn"
EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_MOE = "int4_moe"
EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_NVFP4_MOE = "nvfp4_moe"
EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_LM_HEAD = "lm_head"
EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_ALL = "all"
EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_KINDS = (
    EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_FFN,
    EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_MOE,
    EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_NVFP4_MOE,
    EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_LM_HEAD,
)
EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_CHOICES = (*EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_KINDS, EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_ALL)


def resolve_externalize_weights(externalize_weights) -> "list[str]":
    """Normalize and validate requested external weight kinds."""
    if externalize_weights is None:
        return []
    if isinstance(externalize_weights, str):
        values = [externalize_weights]
    else:
        values = list(externalize_weights)

    requested: list[str] = []
    for value in values:
        if value == EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_ALL:
            return list(EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_KINDS)
        if value not in EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_KINDS:
            supported = ", ".join(EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_CHOICES)
            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported external weight kind '{value}'. "
                             f"Supported kinds: {supported}")
        if value not in requested:
            requested.append(value)
    return requested


def patch_external_weight_manifest(
        out_dir: str,
        external_weight_files: "list[dict[str, object]]") -> None:
    """Add external weight file metadata to runtime ``config.json``."""
    config_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "config.json")
    with open(config_path) as f:
        config = json.load(f)
    config["external_weight_files"] = external_weight_files
    with open(config_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
    logger.info("Patched config.json with %d external weight file(s)",
                len(external_weight_files))


def reject_quantized_lm_head_externalization(
        model, model_dir: str,
        requested_external_weights: "list[str]") -> None:
    """Refuse ``--externalize-weights lm_head`` when the LM head is quantized.

    The LM-head externalization path assumes a single dense fp16 weight
    initializer it can move into a safetensors external weight file.  When the
    LM head is quantized (FP8 / NVFP4 / INT4 AWQ / ...), error out here before
    the expensive ONNX export and tell the user to drop ``lm_head`` from
    ``--externalize-weights`` or re-quantize without ``--lm_head_quantization``.

    The check delegates to :func:`config.module_quant_type`, which encodes
    the same lm_head decision :func:`models.linear.make_linear` uses
    (``quant.excluded``, tied embeddings, MIXED_PRECISION layer overrides,
    dominant ``quant_type``), so detection automatically tracks any future
    ``hf_quant_config.json`` shapes that the parser learns to handle.
    """
    if EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_LM_HEAD not in requested_external_weights:
        return

    from .config import QUANT_FP16, module_quant_type

    lm_head_quant = module_quant_type("lm_head", model.config)
    if lm_head_quant == QUANT_FP16:
        return

    location = f" at {model_dir!r}" if model_dir else ""
    raise ValueError(
        f"--externalize-weights lm_head requires an fp16 LM head, but the "
        f"checkpoint{location} has lm_head quantized as {lm_head_quant!r}. "
        "Re-quantize without --lm_head_quantization, or omit 'lm_head' "
        "from --externalize-weights.")


def _is_dense_ffn_int4_weight_name(
        name: str, layer_types: "list[str] | tuple[str, ...]") -> bool:
    """Return True when ``name`` belongs to a dense FFN int4 plugin input.

    The attention Q/K/V/O projections also use Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin; keep
    them embedded in ONNX for now.  Dense decoder FFNs use ``*.mlp.*`` names in
    the default loader path.  Nemotron-H uses ``backbone.layers.N.mixer`` for
    both attention and MLP-only layers, so check ``layer_types`` before marking
    those weights external.
    """
    normalized = name.replace("/", ".").lower()
    ffn_projections = (".gate_proj.", ".up_proj.", ".down_proj.")
    if ".mlp." in normalized and any(p in normalized for p in ffn_projections):
        return True

    match = re.search(
        r"backbone\.layers\.(\d+)\.mixer\.(up_proj|down_proj)\.",
        normalized,
    )
    if match is None:
        return False

    layer_idx = int(match.group(1))
    return layer_idx < len(layer_types) and layer_types[layer_idx] == "mlp"


def _find_int4_ffn_weight_initializers(onnx_model, model) -> "list[str]":
    """Return dense FFN Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin qweight/scale initializers."""
    initializers = {init.name for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer}
    layer_types = tuple(getattr(model.config, "layer_types", ()))

    external_names: list[str] = []
    external_name_set: set[str] = set()
    for node in onnx_model.graph.node:
        if node.domain != "trt_edgellm" or node.op_type != "Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin":
            continue
        if len(node.input) < 3:
            continue
        if not _is_dense_ffn_int4_weight_name(node.input[1], layer_types):
            continue
        for tensor_name in (node.input[1], node.input[2]):
            if tensor_name in initializers and tensor_name not in external_name_set:
                external_names.append(tensor_name)
                external_name_set.add(tensor_name)
    return external_names


def _find_int4_moe_weight_initializers(onnx_model) -> "list[str]":
    """Return Int4MoePlugin qweight initializers, leaving scales embedded."""
    initializers = {init.name for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer}

    external_names: list[str] = []
    external_name_set: set[str] = set()
    for node in onnx_model.graph.node:
        if node.domain != "trt_edgellm" or node.op_type != "Int4MoePlugin":
            continue
        if len(node.input) < 5:
            continue
        for tensor_name in (node.input[2], node.input[4]):
            if tensor_name in initializers and tensor_name not in external_name_set:
                external_names.append(tensor_name)
                external_name_set.add(tensor_name)
    return external_names


def _find_nvfp4_moe_weight_initializers(onnx_model) -> "list[str]":
    """Return NVFP4 MoE plugin initializer inputs."""
    initializers = {init.name for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer}
    plugin_op_types = {"Nvfp4MoePlugin", "NvFP4MoEPluginGeforce"}

    external_names: list[str] = []
    external_name_set: set[str] = set()
    for node in onnx_model.graph.node:
        if node.domain != "trt_edgellm" or node.op_type not in plugin_op_types:
            continue
        for tensor_name in node.input:
            if tensor_name in initializers and tensor_name not in external_name_set:
                external_names.append(tensor_name)
                external_name_set.add(tensor_name)
    return external_names


def _lm_head_weight_candidate_shapes(model) -> "set[tuple[int, ...]]":
    """Return plausible LM-head shapes: actual weight first, then config vocab/hidden fallbacks."""
    shapes: set[tuple[int, ...]] = set()

    lm_head = getattr(model, "lm_head", None)
    weight = getattr(lm_head, "weight", None)
    if weight is not None:
        shapes.add(tuple(int(dim) for dim in weight.shape))

    config = getattr(model, "config", None)
    hidden_size = getattr(config, "hidden_size", None)
    vocab_sizes = [
        getattr(config, "reduced_vocab_size", None),
        # EAGLE3 draft heads project to a reduced draft vocabulary; the ONNX
        # MatMul weight is then (hidden, draft_vocab), which the vocab_size /
        # reduced_vocab_size fallbacks alone do not cover.
        getattr(config, "draft_vocab_size", None),
        getattr(config, "vocab_size", None),
    ]
    for vocab_size in vocab_sizes:
        if hidden_size is None or vocab_size is None:
            continue
        hidden = int(hidden_size)
        vocab = int(vocab_size)
        shapes.add((vocab, hidden))
        shapes.add((hidden, vocab))
    return shapes


def _find_initializer_from_value(
        value_name: str, initializers: dict, producer_by_output: dict,
        candidate_shapes: "set[tuple[int, ...]]") -> str:
    """Trace simple view/cast values back to a candidate initializer."""
    if value_name in initializers:
        init = initializers[value_name]
        if tuple(init.dims) in candidate_shapes:
            return value_name
        return ""

    producer = producer_by_output.get(value_name)
    if producer is None or producer.op_type not in ("Cast", "Identity",
                                                    "Transpose"):
        return ""

    for input_name in producer.input:
        tensor_name = _find_initializer_from_value(input_name, initializers,
                                                   producer_by_output,
                                                   candidate_shapes)
        if tensor_name:
            return tensor_name
    return ""


def _find_lm_head_weight_initializer(onnx_model, model) -> "str | None":
    """Return the ONNX initializer that backs the final LM-head projection."""
    initializers = {init.name: init for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer}
    candidate_shapes = _lm_head_weight_candidate_shapes(model)
    if not candidate_shapes:
        return None

    shape_matches = [
        init.name for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer
        if tuple(init.dims) in candidate_shapes
    ]
    named_matches = [
        name for name in shape_matches
        if "lm_head" in name.replace("/", ".").lower()
    ]
    if len(named_matches) == 1:
        return named_matches[0]
    if len(shape_matches) == 1:
        return shape_matches[0]

    producer_by_output = {
        output_name: node
        for node in onnx_model.graph.node
        for output_name in node.output
    }

    # Start at the public logits output.  Exporters commonly insert a final
    # Cast or Identity, so walk through those before looking for the Gemm/MatMul
    # that implements the LM-head projection.
    logits_outputs = [
        output.name for output in onnx_model.graph.output
        if output.name == "logits"
    ]
    worklist = logits_outputs or [
        output.name for output in onnx_model.graph.output
    ]
    seen_values: set[str] = set()
    # LogSoftmax appears between logits and the head MatMul for EAGLE3 draft
    # models (which emit log-probs); treat it as a passthrough so the walk
    # reaches the projection weight.
    passthrough_ops = {
        "Cast", "Identity", "Reshape", "Transpose", "LogSoftmax"
    }

    while worklist:
        value_name = worklist.pop()
        if value_name in seen_values:
            continue
        seen_values.add(value_name)

        node = producer_by_output.get(value_name)
        if node is None:
            continue

        # For the final projection, the weight is normally the second Gemm or
        # MatMul input.  It can either be an initializer directly or the input
        # to a small Transpose inserted by ONNX export for torch.nn.Linear.
        if node.op_type in ("Gemm", "MatMul"):
            for input_name in list(node.input)[1:]:
                tensor_name = _find_initializer_from_value(
                    input_name, initializers, producer_by_output,
                    candidate_shapes)
                if tensor_name:
                    return tensor_name
            continue

        if node.op_type in passthrough_ops:
            worklist.extend(node.input[:1])
            continue

        if node.op_type == "Add":
            worklist.extend(input_name for input_name in node.input
                            if input_name not in initializers)

    if len(named_matches) > 1:
        logger.warning(
            "Multiple lm_head-shaped initializers matched by name: %s",
            named_matches)
    elif len(shape_matches) > 1:
        logger.warning("Multiple LM-head-shaped initializers found: %s",
                       shape_matches)
    return None


def _write_external_weight_file(out_dir: str, external_weight_file: str,
                                tensor_names: "list[str]",
                                initializers: dict) -> str:
    """Write selected ONNX initializers to a safetensors external weight file."""
    import numpy as np
    import onnx
    import torch
    from safetensors.torch import save_file

    external_weight_tensors = {}
    for tensor_name in tensor_names:
        init = initializers[tensor_name]
        array = onnx.numpy_helper.to_array(init)
        external_weight_tensors[tensor_name] = torch.from_numpy(
            np.array(array, copy=True))
    external_weight_path = os.path.join(out_dir, external_weight_file)
    save_file(external_weight_tensors, external_weight_path)
    return external_weight_path


def _add_external_weight_inputs(onnx_model, tensor_names: "list[str]",
                                initializers: dict) -> None:
    """Expose selected initializers as fixed-shape ONNX graph inputs."""
    import onnx

    graph_input_names = {inp.name for inp in onnx_model.graph.input}
    for tensor_name in tensor_names:
        if tensor_name not in graph_input_names:
            init = initializers[tensor_name]
            onnx_model.graph.input.append(
                onnx.helper.make_tensor_value_info(
                    tensor_name,
                    init.data_type,
                    list(init.dims),
                ))
            graph_input_names.add(tensor_name)


def _remove_externalized_initializers(onnx_model,
                                      tensor_names: "list[str]") -> None:
    """Remove selected tensors from graph initializers after adding inputs."""
    external_name_set = set(tensor_names)
    retained_initializers = [
        init for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer
        if init.name not in external_name_set
    ]
    del onnx_model.graph.initializer[:]
    onnx_model.graph.initializer.extend(retained_initializers)


def _save_externalized_onnx_model(onnx_path: str, onnx_model) -> None:
    """Save ONNX after all requested externalization passes are complete."""
    import onnx

    out_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(onnx_path))
    ext_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "model.onnx.data")
    if os.path.isfile(ext_path):
        old_size = os.path.getsize(ext_path)
        logger.info("Removing stale external data %s (%.2f GB) before re-save",
                    ext_path, old_size / 1e9)
        os.remove(ext_path)
    onnx.save_model(
        onnx_model,
        onnx_path,
        save_as_external_data=True,
        all_tensors_to_one_file=True,
        location="model.onnx.data",
        convert_attribute=True,
    )


def externalize_model_weights(
        onnx_path: str,
        model,
        externalize_weights=None) -> "list[dict[str, object]]":
    """Move requested ONNX initializer weights into external weight files."""
    import onnx

    requested = resolve_externalize_weights(externalize_weights)
    if not requested:
        return []

    onnx_model = onnx.load(onnx_path)
    initializers = {init.name: init for init in onnx_model.graph.initializer}
    out_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(onnx_path))

    manifest: list[dict[str, object]] = []
    external_names: list[str] = []
    external_name_set: set[str] = set()

    def add_external_weight_file(kind: str, external_weight_file: str,
                                 tensor_names: "list[str]",
                                 manifest_kind: str) -> None:
        new_names = [
            name for name in tensor_names if name not in external_name_set
        ]
        if not new_names:
            return
        external_weight_path = _write_external_weight_file(
            out_dir, external_weight_file, new_names, initializers)
        external_names.extend(new_names)
        external_name_set.update(new_names)
        manifest.append({
            "file": external_weight_file,
            "kind": manifest_kind,
            "tensors": new_names,
        })
        logger.info("Externalized %d %s tensor(s) to %s", len(new_names), kind,
                    external_weight_path)

    for kind in requested:
        if kind == EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_FFN:
            names = _find_int4_ffn_weight_initializers(onnx_model, model)
            if not names:
                logger.warning(
                    "External int4_ffn weights requested, but no dense FFN "
                    "Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin initializers were found")
                continue
            add_external_weight_file("FFN Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin",
                                     "external_int4_ffn_weights.safetensors",
                                     names, "int4_ffn_weights")
            continue

        if kind == EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_INT4_MOE:
            names = _find_int4_moe_weight_initializers(onnx_model)
            if not names:
                logger.warning("External int4_moe weights requested, but no "
                               "Int4MoePlugin qweight initializers were found")
                continue
            add_external_weight_file("Int4MoePlugin qweight",
                                     "external_int4_moe_weights.safetensors",
                                     names, "int4_moe_weights")
            continue

        if kind == EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_NVFP4_MOE:
            names = _find_nvfp4_moe_weight_initializers(onnx_model)
            if not names:
                logger.warning(
                    "External nvfp4_moe weights requested, but no "
                    "NVFP4 MoE plugin initializer inputs were found")
                continue
            add_external_weight_file("NVFP4 MoE plugin",
                                     "external_nvfp4_moe_weights.safetensors",
                                     names, "nvfp4_moe_weights")
            continue

        if kind == EXTERNAL_WEIGHT_LM_HEAD:
            tensor_name = _find_lm_head_weight_initializer(onnx_model, model)
            if tensor_name is None:
                logger.warning(
                    "External lm_head weight requested, but no unique "
                    "LM-head initializer was found")
                continue
            add_external_weight_file("LM-head weight",
                                     "external_lm_head_weight.safetensors",
                                     [tensor_name], "lm_head_weight")

    if not external_names:
        return []

    _add_external_weight_inputs(onnx_model, external_names, initializers)
    _remove_externalized_initializers(onnx_model, external_names)
    _save_externalized_onnx_model(onnx_path, onnx_model)
    return manifest

content.content:
1	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) 2025-2026 NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
2	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
3	#
